pde_description: A 2D reaction-diffusion model for transmembrane potential (u) and gating variables (v, w). \\ \\ \begin{cases} \partial_t u = D \nabla^2 u - \frac{I_{fi}(u, v) + I_{so}(u) + I_{si}(u, w)}{C_m} \\ \partial_t v = (1-H(u-V_c))\frac{1-v}{\tau_{mv}(u)} + H(u-V_c)\frac{-v}{\tau_{pv}} \\ \partial_t w = (1-H(u-V_c))\frac{1-w}{\tau_{mw}} + H(u-V_c)\frac{-w}{\tau_{pw}} \end{cases} \\ \\ \text{where } H(\cdot) \text{ is the Heaviside step function and:} \\ I_{fi}(u, v) = \frac{-v \cdot H(u - V_c) \cdot (u - V_c) \cdot (1 - u)}{\tau_d} \\ I_{so}(u) = \frac{u(1 - H(u - V_c))}{\tau_0} + \frac{H(u - V_c)}{\tau_r} \\ I_{si}(u, w) = \frac{-w(1 + \tanh(k(u - V_{csi})))}{2\tau_{si}} \\ \tau_{mv}(u) = (1 - H(u - V_v))\tau_{v1} + H(u - V_v)\tau_{v2}
notes: Use 2nd order central differences for spatial discretization. Boundary conditions must be enforced at every time step. Consider using internal time-stepping smaller than the output dt for stability.
